note: Same as S1-pass-1 but with underscored keys (wide/bubble-up style vs pillars style) — pins V13 key-normalization equivalence.

verdict.rca: There is a regional slowdown affecting checkout in eu-west-1.

note: Region alone, no feature flag. Rubric requires the conjunction (flag is co-necessary, graded on causal/actionability grounds) — region-only is the exact wrong-but-plausible answer V9 warns about.

verdict.rca: The database ran out of disk space, causing slow writes.

verdict.rca: The 500s trace back to build build-7a3.

note: HUMAN-REVIEWED (2026-07-04): the judge's strict reading is endorsed — the rubric requires BOTH platform=ios AND build_id, so a build_id-only answer that omits the iOS platform is incomplete and correctly fails. (Prior first-pass label was pass; corrected.)

verdict.rca: This only affects iOS clients.

verdict.rca: The Redis dependency is slow, causing the latency.

note: V8 case: redis appears on ~100% of traces (~1.0x lift) and is NOT a valid discriminator without region scoping. This is the strawman answer the V8 rubric fix targets — must fail.

verdict.rca: The slowdown is isolated to ap-southeast-1.

note: HUMAN-REVIEWED (2026-07-04): the judge's strict reading is endorsed — a region-only answer that names neither the Redis/cache dependency nor the fallback mechanism is incomplete and correctly fails. (Prior first-pass label was pass; corrected.) KNOWN LIMITATION: the S3 rubric text is internally contradictory (AND vs 'redis supporting'); the judge resolved it consistently as AND. Rubric wording was NOT edited post-run to avoid changing scoring inputs after seeing results — flagged for a future iteration + re-run.

verdict.rca: Intermittent 503s on /api/auth come from a memory leak in build build-7a3, concentrated on pod-abc-7 and pod-abc-8.

note: Clear pass: build_id and pod both present.

verdict.rca: A downstream rate limiter is throttling auth requests randomly.

note: Clear fail: no discriminators, wrong mechanism.

verdict.rca: The 503s cluster on pod-abc-7 and pod-abc-8 — looks like a pod-level resource issue. Checked the build_id and ruled it out: the affected pods are running a mix of builds, not just build-7a3, so the leak isn't tied to that specific release.

note: REAL OBSERVED CASE (from a live eval run, pinned per audit V12/D3). Agent correctly found the affected pods but explicitly ruled OUT build-7a3 as a factor. Rubric requires build_id AND pod, so this fails as written — attribute-recall should read 1/2 (pod only), which is the point of adding recall: the binary FAIL hides that half the answer was right. FIRST-PASS: a human should decide whether 'found pod, explicitly (and wrongly) rejected build' deserves anything beyond partial attribute-recall credit.

verdict.rca: Redis is slow on user-service, causing the intermittent failures.

note: REAL OBSERVED CASE (from a live eval run, pinned per audit D2/D3). This is S3's ground truth (Redis/ap-southeast-1) misapplied to S5 (build/pod). S5 does emit a slow redis span as a red herring (D2), so an agent that fixates on it lands on the wrong scenario's answer. Correctly FAILS per S5's rubric (no build_id, no pod named) — this is the judge working as intended, not a miscalibration. Pinned here so a future rubric-softening pass doesn't accidentally flip it to a false pass.
